Aggregated bias · 1-glance synthesis
Today's verdict NEUTRAL-CAUTIOUS
Reconciliation · why the tape contradicts itself
⚠ Signals disagree — Crash Index **2/10** (WATCH, calm price action) vs VIX Spike Tier **2/4** MEDIUM with **60%** probability, SKEW and backwardation both active
Realized price is fine, but the options market is paying up for tail protection and the term structure is inverted. Trust the VIX Spike read for forward risk; the Crash Index is backward-looking on price.
⚠ Signals disagree — Regime State score **13** STABLE vs Divergence composite in TOPPING zone, **62.8%** of pairs diverging, Mother Jaws **100**
Headline regime model still says stable because levels have not broken, while the internal correlation map has already shifted. Trust the divergence picture; it leads regime breaks by weeks.
⚠ Signals disagree — US leader ^DJI **-0.8%** (5d) vs US laggard ^NDX **-5.1%** (5d)
Defensive cap-weighted Dow holding while Nasdaq cracks is a classic late-cycle rotation, not a healthy tape. Trust the NDX read on risk appetite.
Today's tension
Price is calm, but options and cross-asset correlations are screaming regime change.
Bull
The Crash Index sits at **2/10** with no active confluences, and regime state scores **13** as STABLE. Nothing in the realized tape says break.
Bear
VIX Spike probability is **60%** at MEDIUM tier, divergence composite is in the TOPPING zone with **62.8%** of pairs diverging, and the Mother Jaws score is pinned at **100**. That is what a vol expansion looks like before it prints.
The tell: Watch whether any of the three T1 confluences (HYG, B2 small-cap, JPY) flips active. One trip and the calm verdict is gone.
AI read · Citrini · today's macro tape

The tape is calm on the surface and rotting underneath. Crash Index prints 2/10 with zero active confluences and the regime model scores 13 STABLE, so by price alone you have nothing to do. The options market disagrees: VIX Spike sits at MEDIUM tier 2/4 with a 60% probability, SKEW is active, and the VIX curve is in backwardation. That is the central tension today.

For positioning, you stay long but you stop being cute. Contrarian reads 50 (ACCUMULATE, partial signal) and macro regime is BROADENING at 30% confidence, so this is not the moment to chase the leaders that have already broken (NDX -5.1% on 5d) or to add gross into a divergence composite running 62.8% bearish bias. Trim the most crowded longs, raise cash, and buy convex downside while SKEW is still being offered. The Dow holding at -0.8% while NDX bleeds tells you the rotation trade is the cleanest expression here.

The main risk is that you read calm price as permission and get caught when one of the T1 confluences flips. The reading that flips the stance is any of T1 x HYG, T1 x B2, or T1 x JPY going active. Currently all three are inactive (0 confluences live). One trip and the WATCH label becomes ALERT inside a session.

5-regime heuristic classifier · cross-asset
Macro Regime — where are we structurally
AI read · macro regime

Regime is BROADENING at 30% confidence, with bias MIXED and contrarian at 50 (ACCUMULATE, partial). Broadening at low confidence means participation is widening from the mega-cap leaders to the rest of the market, but the model is not sure it sticks. You position by rotating, not by adding gross. Take from the leaders that have broken (NDX type exposure) and add to laggards and equal-weight expressions that benefit if breadth genuinely improves. If confidence climbs from 30% toward 60%+ you press the rotation; if it fades and regime flips, you reverse fast.

Dow Theory generalized · cross-asset divergences
Cross-asset divergence radar — Cross-asset divergence watch
AI read · divergences in plain English

The internals have already rolled even though the index print has not. Divergence composite sits in the TOPPING zone with 62.8% of 43 pairs diverging, net bias +11 bearish (19 bearish vs 8 bullish), and Mother Jaws at 100. In plain English: a divergence means two assets that normally move together (or normally move opposite) have stopped doing so. Correlation drift is the size of that shift over a recent window. When vix3m_vix drifts from -0.32 to -0.83, the front of the VIX curve is now violently disconnected from the back. When mtum_spy goes from -0.51 to +0.87, momentum stocks have stopped leading and are now just tracking the tape. When xle_gld flips from -0.81 to +0.22, energy and gold are moving together, which is a stagflation tell. Six pairs at the 100 ceiling at once is not noise; the correlation map is breaking.

Cross-asset confluence · live status
Confluences — most proven alpha of the framework 0/3 ACTIVE
AI read · what the confluences mean now

Zero of the three flagship confluences are active, and that is the read. T1 x HYG (vol backwardation plus credit stress), T1 x B2 (vol backwardation plus small-cap drawdown), and T1 x JPY (vol backwardation plus yen safe-haven flow) are all dark. You have one leg of each (backwardation is on), but credit, small caps, and the yen are not yet confirming. That means you treat today as pre-confluence: position for the setup, not the trigger. Keep hedges on because the vol leg is live, but do not press shorts until HYG widens, IWM cracks, or USDJPY drops. The first confluence to flip is your green light.

✅
T1 × HYG (VIX backwardation + Credit stress)
Volatility backwardation meeting credit stress — the highest-conviction pre-correction setup in the framework. Fires only when options fear and the bond market confirm each other.

🌍
Asia · Europe · US · Crypto 24/7 — lead-lag detection
Cross-Region Surveillance
Major indices from the 4 regions/asset classes. Asia trades ahead time-wise vs US — a Tokyo overnight crash often leaks into the SPX open. Crypto = 24h leading indicator of global risk appetite.
AI read · the cross-region tape

The 24/7 tape has a clean bearish lean. Asia: ^KS11 -11.8% on 5d is a serious crack, with ^AXJO holding at -1.0% as the regional leader. Europe is split, ^SSMI +2.0% leading on defensive Swiss bid while ^GDAXI -2.5% lags on cyclical pain. The US already shows the same pattern with defensive ^DJI -0.8% above growth-heavy ^NDX -5.1%. Crypto is the only risk venue still bid: ETH +5.5% and BTC +3.1% on 5d. Defensives leading on three continents while crypto holds tells you the equity rotation is the dominant flow, not a full risk-off. For the US session, fade strength in growth and beta, lean into low-vol and large-cap defensives, and treat any crypto wobble as the confirmation that the last risk leg is folding.

01
Forward-looking indicators · Bonferroni-validated
Crash Index — 10 lead-signal radar 2/10 · WATCH — 1-2 signals, monitoring
AI read · volatility & crash, reconciled

Crash Index at 2/10 and VIX Spike at tier 2/4 with 60% probability is a coherent state, not a bug. The Crash Index measures realized stress in price, breadth, and credit; none of that has broken, so it correctly reads WATCH. The VIX Spike Detector measures what the options market is paying to hedge: SKEW active means out-of-the-money puts are bid relative to calls, and backwardation active means near-dated VIX futures are above longer-dated ones. Both are forward-looking signals of expected vol expansion. You get this configuration when smart hedgers position ahead of a catalyst the cash tape has not priced yet. Trust the options market here; realized always catches up to implied when the term structure is inverted.

CRASH INDEX
2/10
WATCH — 1-2 signals, monitoring
10 forward-looking indicators · Bonferroni-validated
DXY z>1.5 structural
lead 30-90d ❌ z=+1.41
DXY+SKEW combo structural
lead 45-346d ❌
SKEW>140 (Tail Risk) structural
lead 1-94d ✅ 142.0
SKEW>155 structural
current regime ❌ 142.0
VIX3M/VIX<1.0 stress
lead 5-20d ❌ 1.07
STLFSI4 d20 stress
lead 5-30d ❌ -0.22
HYG/IEF z<-1.5 stress
lead 5-20d ❌ z=+1.07
T10Y2Y resteepen stress
recession warning ❌ +0.00
SPY -2% in 5d (breakdown) trigger
lead 5-15d 🔻 -2.96%
VIX +30% in 3d trigger
lead 1-10d ❌ -2.60%
Score 0-2 CALM · 3-4 WATCH · 5-6 WARNING · 7-8 ALARM · 9-10 EXTREME (rare crash imminent). Lead times: structural 30-90d · stress 5-30d · triggers 1-10d. Four Telegram alerts available depending on escalation.
How to read
Each row = an independent signal Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY. The structural (DXY, SKEW, MOVE) signal a regime change over 30-90 days. The stress (HYG/IEF, VIX3M/VIX, STLFSI4) ring at 5-30 days. The triggers (SPY -2% in 5d, VIX +30% in 3d) are short-term breakouts. For a tactical decision: 3+ signals simultaneously aligned = real probability of stress, never on isolated signal (framework rule).

How to read
Score 0-100 aggregated from 6 sub-signals (each 0-20) that predict SPY underperformance. Bonferroni-validated over 15 years, walk-forward robust. Above 50 → active-bearish regime (trim winners, tight stops). DXY surge added in May 2026 (current regime 2023-2026, strong dollar = toxic for global risk-on).

🆕 SMH BTFD (regime current) 0/50
SMH/SPY Z=+1.60 (<-1.5 → +11% 60d test 2023-26)
Contrarian Score = signals that predict SPY OUTPERFORMANCE (capitulation buy). VIX backwardation → +7.29pp SPY at 120d · Corr SPY-TLT>0 → +3.88pp at 120d · SMH z<-1.5 → +11pp at 60d (test 2023-2026). Reading: score > 70 = aggressive fear-buy setup.
How to read
Bonferroni MCPT backtest 2026-04-30 over 15 years SPY · 9/15 tests VALID. Regime Score vs Contrarian Score are independent — they can coexist high (regime transition) or low (structural calm). Always read the combination, not each one alone.

Probability VIX +50% within 60d
60%
vs baseline 37% · backtested cycle 2023-2026
Action — sizing tied to tier
Buy hedges B-tier (1-2% book) — VIX calls 30-60 DTE strike 25-28 OR SPY puts 60-90 DTE Δ0.20-0.30
How to read
4 tiers (BROAD / MEDIUM / HIGH / EXTREME) based on the confluence of 3 Validated components: SKEW > 140 (tail-hedge demand), VIX3M/VIX > 1.05 (vol curve inverted), VIX < 16 + ratio > 1.15 (adaptive proxy). Each tier indicates a historical probability of VIX +50% at 60d (cycle backtest 2023-2026, vs baseline 37%). Strict sizing: tier 2 = 1-2% book hedges, tier 3 = 3-5% long-vol, tier 4 = 5-10% MAX (rare confluence).
05b
Under-the-hood vol · Observation (Imran Lakha)
Vol Microstructure — VIX beta & term structure
VIX spot
21.06
VIX beta 20d
4.19
92th pct
VIX beta 60d
1.13
65th pct
VIX9D / VIX
1.135
backwardation
VIX3M / VIX
1.056
contango
VVIX / VIX
4.96
vol-of-vol
VIX beta 20d high (4.19, 92th pct) — VIX very reactive; vol rich to the move.
How to read
VIX beta = how much VIX moves per unit SPX move (rolling regression). Collapsed beta (low pct) = VIX asleep → historically a buy, but worthless unless VIX re-engages on the downside. VIX9D/VIX > 1 & VIX3M/VIX < 1 = backwardation (stress). Observation only — no sized signal.

Aggregated bias · 53-ratio composite
Regime synthesis — RISK-ON / OFF / STRESS / TOPPING
MIXED — competing signals No clean composite per Kerberos. Wait for alignment.
RISK-ON aligned (2)
• Small caps leading
• Copper > Gold
RISK-OFF aligned (1)
• ETH dumping vs BTC
STRESS aligned (0)
none
TOPPING aligned (0)
none
How to read
Aggregation of all panel signals across the 4 cross-asset buckets. RISK-ON = risk appetite (cyclical, equity, EM). RISK-OFF = flight to defensives (TLT, gold, JPY). STRESS = liquidity tension (HYG, MOVE, STLFSI). TOPPING = end-of-cycle signals (concentration, breadth narrowing, defensives leading). Doctrine: a single signal = noise. 3+ aligned in one bucket = real confirmed regime.

07
VOL TERM STRUCTURE · 5 ratios
Volatility — VIX-family ratios
VIX3M / VIX
Stretch -0.51
Volatility term structure
1.0725 -2.40%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
This ratio compares 3-month fear (VIX3M) to immediate fear (VIX). Above 1, traders anticipate more stress in 3 months than today — that's the normal situation. Below 1, it's the opposite: immediate panic more expensive than long-term insurance. Current reading: 1.07 — normal calm regime. The curve is in typical contango, neither panic nor excessive complacency. Action: no directional signal, watch for a break below 1.00 (panic) or above 1.30 (top warning).
VVIX / VIX
Stretch -1.10
Vol of vol
4.822 -1.27%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
VVIX = volatility of the VIX itself. When it rises sharply, it means institutionals are actively buying insurance against a crash (deep puts, VIX calls). It's an internal stress signal from the options market. Current reading: Z=-1.10 — no particular stress in options. Average insurance level, neither complacency nor panic. Action: no directional reading, follow the other signals.
VIX9D / VIX
Stretch +2.35
Ultra short-term panic
1.1142 +7.07%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
VIX9D = 9-day fear, VIX = 30-day fear. When the 9-day exceeds the 30-day, it means an immediate shock is anticipated (often around an event: Fed, election, earnings). Current reading: 1.11 — VIX 9-day above 30-day, imminent shock priced. The market expects turbulence within the week. Action: don't chase rallies, keep cash to buy on the dip if the shock materializes.
SKEW
Stretch -0.27
Black swan insurance
142.0 -2.09%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
The SKEW index measures how much institutionals pay to protect against an extreme SPX crash (puts far out of the money, what's called 'tail-hedge'). Above 140, demand for protection is strong. Current reading: 142 — moderate demand zone for crash insurance. Big players are starting to hedge, no panic yet. Action: watch for a move above 150 = full alarm, otherwise stay neutral.
SKEW / VIX
Stretch -0.21
Tail-risk premium relative to vol
7.14 -6.77%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
SKEW/VIX compares the cost of extreme crash insurance to the cost of standard volatility insurance. When this ratio rises, it means tail fear is higher than daily fear — an interesting divergence. Current reading: Z=-0.21 — standard level, no gap. Tail insurance cost aligned with normal vol. Action: no signal.
VIX / Realized Vol 30d
Stretch +0.17
Vol regime persistence — chop vs crash detector
1.66 +4.54%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
Z=+0.17, last value 1.6577799326501823. No specific Kerberos reading defined for this pair.

Credit & rates — risk-on/off proxies
HYG / IEF
Stretch +1.07
High yield credit stress
0.8482 -0.09%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
HYG = high-yield bonds (junk bonds), IEF = US sovereign bonds 7-10 years. The ratio measures the health of risky credit: when HYG underperforms IEF, smart money is fleeing credit risk before it hits equities. Current reading: Z=+1.07 — balanced credit, no junk stress. The bond market is flagging nothing unusual. Action: no defensive reading from credit.
LQD / IEF
Stretch +1.11
Investment grade liquidity
1.1544 -0.13%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
LQD = investment-grade corporate bonds, IEF = 7-10y treasuries. When even quality bonds underperform treasuries, it means liquidity is tightening everywhere, not just on junk. Current reading: Z=+1.11 — quality credit stable. Investment-grade spreads healthy, no macro liquidity stress. Action: no defensive signal from quality credit.

TIP / TLT
Stretch +0.48
Inflation expectations
1.2866 +0.18%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
TIPS (inflation-indexed bonds) divided by TLT (long nominal bonds). When TIPS outperforms TLT, the bond market is pricing more inflation to come — risk for equity multiples. Current reading: Z=+0.48 — stable inflation expectations. No inflation panic priced. Action: no signal on multiples.
MOVE
Stretch +0.25
Bond volatility (BofA)
77.0 +0.06%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
MOVE = implied volatility of Treasury bonds (VIX equivalent for bonds). When the bond market becomes volatile, it's often a precursor to equity stress (bond vol leads equity vol). Current reading: 77 — normal bond vol. No Treasury market stress. Action: no defensive signal from bonds.
Corr SPY / TLT 60d
Stretch +1.82
Equity-bond regime
0.54 -3.38%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
60-day rolling correlation between SPY (equities) and TLT (long bonds). Deflation regime = negative correlation (bonds = haven, play their hedging role). Inflation regime = positive correlation (both fall together — risk parity under stress). Current reading: +0.54 — inflation regime active. Bonds and equities fall together, the traditional 60/40 no longer works as a hedge. Action: don't count on TLT to hedge SPY, look for other protections (gold, VIX calls, cash).

Consumer confidence
1.3275 -3.65%

XLY = consumer discretionary (cyclical, discretionary purchases), XLP = consumer staples (defensive, necessary purchases). When XLP beats XLY, the consumer is going into cautious mode — often a bearish divergence if SPX keeps rising. Current reading: Z=-0.95 — balanced discretionary/staples. No defensive rotation. Action: no sector signal.
IWM / SPY
Stretch +1.28
Risk appetite (small vs large)
0.3888 +0.54%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
IWM = Russell 2000 (small caps), SPY = S&P 500 (large caps). When small caps underperform strongly, it means breadth is dying — the rally is only carried by mega-caps. Classic end-of-cycle signal. Current reading: Z=+1.28 — normal breadth. Small and large caps balanced. Action: no signal.
SMH / SPY
Stretch +1.60
Tech leadership
0.7870 -1.85%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
SMH = semis ETF (Nvidia, AMD, etc.), SPY = S&P 500. When semis dominate to the extreme, it's often an exhaustion signal (tech can't outperform indefinitely). When they break down, it's the end of the tech leadership cycle. Current reading: Z=+1.60 — tech leadership intact, not extreme. Action: no rotation signal.
SPY / RSP
Stretch +0.34
Cap-weighted vs equal-weighted
3.5125 -0.31%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
SPY = S&P 500 cap-weighted (mega-caps weigh enormously), RSP = S&P 500 equal-weighted (each stock 0.2%). When SPY outperforms RSP, mega-caps are hiding the weakness of the rest — dangerous concentration. Current reading: Z=+0.34 — broad market participation. No dangerous concentration. Action: no signal.

Stretch +0.66
Offensive vs defensive
3.8559 -3.43%

EV% = win_rate × (target−entry) − (1−win_rate) × (entry−stop). Tier = T1 highest conviction. 52w% = % of 52-week high. Entry/stop/target levels live on the Trade Desk →
How to read
Heat ranking: composite of RS (relative strength vs SPY), Momentum (price action 20-60d), Breadth (% stocks above SMA), Options flow, and Attention. Use top-5 as rotation focus.
Emerging industries: high velocity = screener scores accelerating this week vs prior baseline — potential early rotation signal.
Competitor themes: token frequency × recency across top finance Substacks — useful narrative context, not a trading signal.
Best R/R: only entries with an active screener leg (PULLBACK / BOTTOM / SMART_MONEY_SURGE etc.). EV% = probabilistic edge per trade. Cross-check with weekly chart before sizing.

Reading the table — Z-Score: number of standard deviations vs rolling 100d mean. |Z| > 2 = Kerberos entry threshold, |Z| > 2.5 = extreme (best R/R on mean-reversion). %B: position within Bollinger Bands (1.0 = upper band, 0 = lower band).

Aggregate reading — the mean of Z-scores gives a global regime indicator. Positive → cross-asset overbought market (Risk-On, watch reversal). Negative → oversold (Risk-Off, possible bottoming).
How to read
Persist = number of consecutive days above |Z| > 2.0 (Kerberos threshold). Persist ≥ 3d = validated signal (the divergence has become a regime, not a spike). Persist 1-2d = to watch. Persist 0d = dead signal. Sort on this column to find established regimes worth trading.
Glossary — 18 terms to understand the dashboard
Z-Score
Number of standard deviations vs the historical mean (rolling 100 days). Z=0 = normal level · |Z|>2 = stress · |Z|>2.5 = extreme.
Bollinger Bands
Bands drawn at ±2.2σ around a 100-day moving average. When the price exits the bands = statistically extreme signal.
Backwardation
Inverted curve: immediate fear exceeds far fear (e.g. VIX > VIX3M). Short-term panic signal.
Contango
Normal situation where far fear exceeds immediate fear. Extreme contango = complacency.
Half-life
Number of days required for a gap to the mean to halve. Measures the speed of mean-reversion.
Cointegration
Two assets that move together in the long term despite short-term fluctuations. Statistical basis of pair-trading.
Mean-reversion
Strategy betting that a gap to the mean will close. Inverse of momentum (which bets on continuation).
Breadth
Width of participation in the rally. Good breadth = many stocks rising. Bad breadth = only a few mega-caps pull the index.
Hedge ratio
Quantity of asset B to short to neutralize 1 long unit of asset A. Calculated via OLS regression on historical returns.
Lead-time
Delay between a signal's appearance and the event it predicts. Structural = 30-90d · stress = 5-30d · triggers = 1-10d.
Bonferroni
Statistical correction for multiple testing. When testing 100 signals, some seem valid by chance. Bonferroni divides the significance threshold by the number of tests to eliminate these false positives.
MCPT (Monte Carlo Permutation Test)
Robustness test: data is randomly shuffled 5000-10000 times to see if the signal appears by chance. If p < 0.05, the signal is probably real.
Walk-forward
Test where the model is trained on the first years (e.g. 2010-2020) and tested on the following years (2020-2025). Avoids in-sample overfitting.
Tail-hedge
Hedge against an extreme crash (-20% or more). Bought via deeply out-of-the-money puts. SKEW measures institutional demand for this type of insurance.
Mega-caps
The 7-10 largest market caps in the SPX (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA). Weigh ~35% of the index.
Cap-weighted vs Equal-weighted
SPY = weighted by capitalization (mega-caps dominate). RSP = each stock weighs 0.2%. Comparing both reveals concentration.
Risk parity
Portfolio strategy that balances risk (not capital) across asset classes. Stressed when stocks/bonds correlations turn positive (inflation regime).
framework rule
Fundamental principle: a solo signal = noise, 3+ aligned signals = real edge. All panel signals are Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY.
